description: Phase 1 — Schema & Rule Management API: DB migrations for all 4 platform_automation tables, repository layer, rule DSL validator, and full REST API for rule CRUD, versioning, and lifecycle management.

userStories.title: Service scaffold — apps/platform/automation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Automation Engine service directory initialised with correct project files so subsequent stories have a working build target.
acceptanceCriteria: Create directory tree: apps/platform/automation/src/{routes,services,repositories,events}/, migrations/, test/unit/, test/integration/

package.json: name @platform/automation, type module (ESM), engines.node >=24, deps: fastify@^5, @fastify/sensible, knex@^3, pg, @sinclair/typebox@^0.34, bullmq, pino, devDeps: typescript@^5, vitest, @types/node, tsx
tsconfig.json: module NodeNext, moduleResolution NodeNext, strict true, target ES2022, outDir dist, rootDir src
src/index.ts: creates Fastify instance with pino logger, registers a /healthz route returning { ok: true }, calls fastify.listen on PORT env (default 3000)
Dockerfile: node:24-alpine, copies source, runs npm ci --omit=dev, CMD node dist/index.js
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No external deps needed at runtime yet — just scaffolding. ESM throughout (import/export, .js extensions in relative imports).

userStories.title: Database migrations — platform_automation schema (all 4 tables)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Knex migration files that create the full platform_automation schema so the service has its complete DB structure from day one.
acceptanceCriteria: migrations/001_create_automation_rules.ts: creates automation_rules table with columns: id uuid PK, name text NOT NULL, status text NOT NULL DEFAULT 'draft', active_version integer nullable, current_version integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 1, created_by uuid, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(), updated_at timestamptz DEFAULT now()

migrations/002_create_automation_rule_versions.ts: creates automation_rule_versions with: id uuid PK, rule_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES automation_rules(id), version integer NOT NULL, trigger_event_type text NOT NULL, condition jsonb, active_hours jsonb, action_tree jsonb NOT NULL, created_by uuid, created_at timestamptz DEFAULT now(); UNIQUE(rule_id, version); index on trigger_event_type

migrations/003_create_automation_executions.ts: creates automation_executions with: id uuid PK, rule_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES automation_rules(id), rule_version integer NOT NULL, action_tree_snapshot jsonb NOT NULL, event_id text NOT NULL, event_type text NOT NULL, entity_type text, entity_id text, status text NOT NULL, started_at timestamptz, completed_at timestamptz; UNIQUE(event_id, rule_id)

migrations/004_create_automation_execution_steps.ts: creates automation_execution_steps with: id uuid PK, execution_id uuid NOT NULL REFERENCES automation_executions(id), action_type text NOT NULL, action_params jsonb, output jsonb, status text NOT NULL, attempt integer NOT NULL DEFAULT 0, error text, started_at timestamptz, completed_at timestamptz
migrations/knexfile.ts exports default config using DATABASE_URL env with schema: platform_automation, migrationsTableName: knex_migrations
Running knex migrate:latest against a Postgres instance applies all 4 migrations without error
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: All tables in platform_automation schema (set search_path in connection config). Executions/steps tables are used in later phases but must exist now per the phase spec.

userStories.title: Repository layer — rules and rule versions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed Knex repository for automation_rules and automation_rule_versions so route handlers and services have a clean DB access interface.
acceptanceCriteria: src/repositories/rules.repository.ts exports class RulesRepository with constructor(db: Knex)
create(data: CreateRuleInput): Promise<Rule> — inserts into automation_rules (status: 'draft', current_version: 1) and returns the created row
findAll(filters?: { status?: string }): Promise<Rule[]> — returns all non-deleted rules, optional status filter, ordered by created_at DESC
findById(id: string): Promise<Rule | null> — returns rule or null
update(id: string, data: Partial<UpdateRuleInput>): Promise<Rule | null> — updates rule row, sets updated_at, returns updated row or null if not found
softDelete(id: string): Promise<boolean> — sets status = 'deleted', updated_at = now(); returns true if row was found and updated
insertVersion(data: CreateVersionInput): Promise<RuleVersion> — inserts into automation_rule_versions, returns created row
findVersion(ruleId: string, version: number): Promise<RuleVersion | null>
activateVersion(ruleId: string, version: number): Promise<Rule | null> — sets automation_rules.active_version = version, status = 'active', updated_at = now(); returns updated rule
All TypeScript types (Rule, RuleVersion, CreateRuleInput, etc.) defined in src/repositories/rules.repository.ts or a co-located types file
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pure DB access — no validation logic here. Use Knex querybuilder; no raw SQL except where unavoidable.

userStories.title: Rule DSL validator — branch nesting cap and required fields (pure function + unit tests)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure validation function for the rule action_tree DSL so invalid rules are rejected at save time before hitting the DB.
acceptanceCriteria: src/services/rule-validator.ts exports validateActionTree(tree: unknown): ValidationResult where ValidationResult = { valid: true } | { valid: false; errors: string[] }
Rejects action_tree missing type field: error 'action_tree: missing required field type'
Rejects branch node at nesting depth 4 or greater: error 'action_tree: branch nesting exceeds maximum depth of 3'
Accepts branch node at depth 1, 2, and 3 (valid)
Rejects send_message node missing dedup_key: logs warning string (does not return error — per Q&A answer C: worker proceeds without idempotency protection)
Rejects unknown action type: error 'action_tree: unknown action type <value>' for types not in [send_message, send_email, call_ai, enroll_sequence, emit_event, call_webhook, branch]
Validates both if_true and if_false branches of a branch node recursively
test/unit/rule-validator.test.ts covers: valid flat action tree, valid 3-level nested branch, invalid 4-level nested branch, missing type field, unknown action type
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Pure function — no DB, no I/O, no side effects. Branch depth counted from root (root branch node = depth 1). The send_message dedup_key warning is a log warning not a hard error per Q&A Q7 answer C.

userStories.title: Read API routes — GET /rules and GET /rules/:id
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to list and retrieve automation rules so I can display them in the rule management UI.
acceptanceCriteria: src/routes/rules.ts registers GET /rules route on the Fastify instance
GET /rules accepts optional query param ?status= (draft|active|disabled); omitting it returns all non-deleted rules
GET /rules response: 200 array of rule objects with fields: id, name, status, active_version, current_version, created_by, created_at, updated_at
GET /rules/:id returns 200 with single rule object if found, 404 with { error: 'Not found' } if not found or soft-deleted
TypeBox schemas defined for both responses and query params; Fastify serialises using the schemas
Routes registered in src/index.ts via fastify.register(rulesRoutes)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: No auth middleware in Phase 1 — auth is wired later. Soft-deleted rules (status='deleted') must not appear in any list or single-fetch response.

userStories.title: Write routes — POST /rules and DELETE /rules/:id
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to create new draft rules and soft-delete existing ones so I can manage the rule library.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /rules accepts body: { name: string, trigger_event_type: string, condition?: object, active_hours?: object, action_tree: object, created_by?: string }
POST /rules runs validateActionTree on action_tree; returns 422 with { errors: string[] } if invalid
POST /rules on success: inserts automation_rules row (status: 'draft', current_version: 1) and automation_rule_versions row (version: 1), returns 201 with created rule object
DELETE /rules/:id returns 204 on successful soft-delete (status → 'deleted')
DELETE /rules/:id returns 404 if rule not found or already deleted
TypeBox schemas defined for POST body and all responses
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: POST creates both the rule group row and the first version row atomically (use a Knex transaction). active_version stays NULL on creation — rule is draft until explicitly activated.

userStories.title: Edit and lifecycle routes — PUT /rules/:id, PUT /rules/:id/versions/:v/activate, PATCH /rules/:id/status
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want to edit rules, activate specific versions, and toggle rule status so I can manage the full rule lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /rules/:id accepts body: { name?: string, trigger_event_type?: string, condition?: object, active_hours?: object, action_tree?: object }
PUT /rules/:id runs validateActionTree on action_tree if provided; returns 422 if invalid

PUT /rules/:id on success: inserts a new automation_rule_versions row with version = current_version + 1, increments automation_rules.current_version, updates name if provided; returns 200 with updated rule object
PUT /rules/:id returns 404 if rule not found or deleted
PUT /rules/:id/versions/:v/activate: sets automation_rules.active_version = v, status = 'active', updated_at = now(); returns 200 with updated rule
PUT /rules/:id/versions/:v/activate returns 404 if rule or version not found; returns 409 if rule is 'deleted'
PATCH /rules/:id/status accepts body: { status: 'active' | 'disabled' }; updates rule status; returns 200 with updated rule
PATCH /rules/:id/status returns 422 if status is not 'active' or 'disabled'; returns 404 if rule not found or deleted
TypeBox schemas defined for all request bodies and responses
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Activation does not re-validate the action_tree — validation happens only at save time. PATCH /rules/:id/status 'active' is allowed even if active_version is NULL (rule stays active but no version will match — this is a degenerate state the UI should prevent but the API does not block).
